annotations.label: Average (central value)

anchor.snippet: an average of a collection or group is a value that is most central

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the arithmetic average via `Finset.expect` and `MeasureTheory.average`, but does not encode the informal notion of a generic 'most central value'.

annotations.label: Arithmetic mean as average

anchor.snippet: The most commonly used definition of the average is the arithmetic mean

annotations.note: `Finset.expect s f` is defined as `(s.card : ℚ≥0)⁻¹ • ∑ i ∈ s, f i`, i.e. the arithmetic mean of `f` over `s`.

annotations.label: Arithmetic mean of [2,3,4,7,9]

anchor.snippet: so the "average" of the list of numbers

annotations.note: Specific numerical illustration, not a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Three Ms

anchor.snippet: "average" sometimes refers to "the three Ms"

annotations.note: Informal grouping (mean/median/mode) is not codified as a Mathlib object.

annotations.label: Median

anchor.snippet: The median , defined as the value in the center after sorting the group

annotations.note: No `median` of a finite list/multiset is defined anywhere in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Median income

anchor.snippet: the average personal income is usually given as the median income

annotations.note: Real-world example without a Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Harmonic mean

anchor.snippet: The harmonic mean , defined as the reciprocal of the mean of the reciprocals

annotations.note: The harmonic mean appears informally in the HM-GM inequality discussion but is not given a top-level definition.

annotations.label: Harmonic mean of speeds

anchor.snippet: a harmonic mean of speeds will give the value of the constant speed

annotations.note: Word problem illustration, not formalized.

annotations.label: Mode

anchor.snippet: The mode represents the most common value found in the group

annotations.note: No statistical `mode` function is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Other average statistics

anchor.snippet: Other statistics that can be used as an average include the mid-range

annotations.note: Mid-range, midhinge, trimean, etc. are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Averages lie within bounding box

anchor.snippet: All averages of a collection are somewhere within its bounding box

annotations.note: There is no abstract notion of 'average' in Mathlib over which to state this meta-property.

annotations.label: Monotonicity of averages

anchor.snippet: Most averages [ a ] are monotonic

annotations.note: Stated only as a general property of an unformalized class of averages.

annotations.label: Linear homogeneity of averages

anchor.snippet: All commonly-used averages are linearly homogeneous

annotations.note: `Finset.smul_expect` and `MeasureTheory.measure_smul_average` give linear homogeneity for the arithmetic average, but not for averages in general.

annotations.label: Permutation invariance of averages

anchor.snippet: Most averages [ b ] remain identical when the list of items is permuted

annotations.note: `Finset.expect` is symmetric in its arguments by construction (via `expect_bij`), but no general permutation-invariance theorem is stated over an abstract class of averages.

annotations.label: Quantile as optimization

anchor.snippet: th quantile (another summary statistic that generalizes the median) can similarly be expressed as a solution to the optimization problem

annotations.note: No quantile function or its variational characterization exists in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Generalized f-mean

anchor.snippet: one can create their own average metric using the generalized

annotations.note: Mathlib has no generalized/Kolmogorov f-mean definition.

annotations.label: Harmonic and geometric mean as f-means

anchor.snippet: The harmonic mean is an example of this using

annotations.note: Depends on the unformalized f-mean framework.

annotations.label: General average via function g

anchor.snippet: A more general method [ 17 ] [ failed verification ] for defining an average

annotations.note: Mathlib does not provide this `g`-based abstract average construction.

annotations.label: Arithmetic, geometric, harmonic via g

anchor.snippet: provides the arithmetic mean

annotations.note: Specialization of the unformalized general-average construction.

annotations.label: Moving average

anchor.snippet: An easy way to do this is the moving average

annotations.note: No moving/rolling average function is defined in `Mathlib/`.
